content: Episodic memory in neural architecture planning agents can be bootstrapped by self-supervised contrastive pre-training of past interaction trajectories, where unlabeled agent experiences automatically act as both positive and negative training examples to shape more generalizable long-horizon planning. This creates a self-improving cycle where the agent’s accumulated episodic memory simultaneously improves its planning performance and generates new training data for self-supervised learning without human annotation.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
trigger_topic: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
